chain_edges.notes: Indirect prompt injection trend feeds the MCP-protocol attack-surface thesis; both surface as recurring CVE categories.
GGUF supply-chain CVE (SGLang) and MCP-protocol CVEs share the model/agent-protocol-as-attack-surface pattern.
OAuth-scope category for AI extensions extends naturally into MCP-over-mTLS / OAuth scope specs at the protocol layer.
Agent Control Plane governance work overlaps the MCP Gateway / Firewall product surface predicted by the focal.
Agent-in-the-loop secret exfiltration overlaps MCP runtime-security category; both push OWASP-style top-list adoption.
MCP Gateway / Firewall maturation feeds the broader Agent Registry standard for tool permissions and audit traces.
RFP-grade 8h production benchmarks feed directly into IROS/GTC league-table criteria for OEM-adopted humanoid records.
RaaS cloud-training consolidation accelerates fleet metrics that league tables will rank vendors on.
5-pole exclusive lab-cloud alliance regime directly feeds the three-cloud training oligopoly thesis.
Training-vs-inference SKU split underwrites the layered training/inference market shape.
Three-cloud frontier-training capture reinforces closed-weight frontier vs open-weight production split.
Inference-tier specialization by Oracle/IBM/Coreweave dovetails with cloud-hosted Robot-as-a-Service inference.

relations.notes: Both predict CVSS 8+ AI-runtime CVEs as Patch-Tuesday regulars; focal narrows on MCP, sibling on prompt injection.
Parallel protocol-level security baselines: model-file (GGUF) supply chain vs MCP tool-call protocol attack surface.
Parallel auth-control predictions: AI-extension OAuth scope categories vs MCP-over-mTLS / OAuth scope standardization.
Agent Control Plane governance and MCP Gateway/Firewall both target enterprise agent-runtime control; complementary facets.
Both target agent-runtime security categories landing on CIS / OWASP top lists by 2026 Q3.
Parallel security-baseline predictions: third-party-vendor SOC2/FedRAMP vs MCP-protocol mTLS/OAuth.
Agent Registry standard and MCP-protocol gateway both formalize agent-tool distribution and audit; orthogonal layers.
Headless-preferred MCP metadata and MCP-protocol security baselines both expand MCP surface by H2 2026.
League-table publication presupposes the same OEM-adopted production-record metrics that 6f0a names as RFP cuts.
Both forecast Physical AI commercialization in H2 2026 from different facets: ranking/shakeout vs RaaS recurring revenue.
Both describe lab-cloud structural lock-in; this prediction emphasizes capacity oligopoly, the other emphasizes 5-pole exclusivity.
Three-cloud training oligopoly and training-vs-inference SKU split are independent facets of the same hyperscaler silicon stack.
Capacity-driven cloud oligopoly and proprietary closed-weights split share Q3 2026 horizon but cover different layers.
Hyperscaler control-plane race and frontier training-capacity capture are independent but mutually reinforcing oligopoly axes.
Cloud-hosted robot training pipeline and three-cloud frontier-training capture both reflect compute centralization in the same Q3 2026 window.
